raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":1.0,"R1.5":0.7,"R1.6":1.0,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.4","R1.5","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":"","rationale":{"R1.1":"Making the draft stronger centrally involves sharpening the thesis, gap, and contribution claim above the sentence level.","R1.2":"The request implies checking whether the argument from problem motivation to method to results is coherent and persuasive.","R1.3":"Strengthening the draft requires ensuring reported performance claims are appropriately tied to the described benchmarks and comparisons.","R1.4":"The artifact makes a strong first-algorithm claim against named prior work, so novelty positioning is load-bearing.","R1.5":"The intro-level narrative arc and ordering of motivation, prior work, method, and results are central to making the text stronger.","R1.6":"The broad request most directly asks for improved clarity, concision, register, and persuasive prose at sentence and paragraph level.","RX.1":"Any projection must preserve uncertainty about what strengthening entails beyond the stated delegation and artifact.","RX.2":"The projection must account for avoiding exaggerated claims, especially around novelty and empirical reductions.","RX.3":"The responsibility is limited to projecting the delegation rather than editing or evaluating the artifact itself.","RX.4":"The projection should avoid endorsing potentially misleading research claims that could affect downstream scholarly use.","RX.5":"The projection should keep responsibility traceable to explicit features of the delegation and artifact."}}

rationale.R1.1: Making the draft stronger centrally involves sharpening the thesis, gap, and contribution claim above the sentence level.
rationale.R1.2: The request implies checking whether the argument from problem motivation to method to results is coherent and persuasive.
rationale.R1.3: Strengthening the draft requires ensuring reported performance claims are appropriately tied to the described benchmarks and comparisons.
rationale.R1.4: The artifact makes a strong first-algorithm claim against named prior work, so novelty positioning is load-bearing.
rationale.R1.5: The intro-level narrative arc and ordering of motivation, prior work, method, and results are central to making the text stronger.
rationale.R1.6: The broad request most directly asks for improved clarity, concision, register, and persuasive prose at sentence and paragraph level.
rationale.RX.1: Any projection must preserve uncertainty about what strengthening entails beyond the stated delegation and artifact.
rationale.RX.2: The projection must account for avoiding exaggerated claims, especially around novelty and empirical reductions.
rationale.RX.3: The responsibility is limited to projecting the delegation rather than editing or evaluating the artifact itself.
rationale.RX.4: The projection should avoid endorsing potentially misleading research claims that could affect downstream scholarly use.
rationale.RX.5: The projection should keep responsibility traceable to explicit features of the delegation and artifact.
